title: 3D Printed Nasopharyngeal Swabs with Wrapped Rayon Fibers
author: Justin Ryan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bemxjc7n

steps.title: Print the SCREEN design in a...
steps.instruction: Print the SCREEN design in a nylon-based media and ensure additional adhesives or binding agents are not used unless additional validation is performed.

steps.title: While wearing gloves, open the supplied 3D print file
steps.instruction: While wearing gloves, open the supplied 3D print file. SCREEEN_NPSwab_Rev56 (1).stl Note the following: The file was designed to not need support structures in the case of material extrusion Specific printers may still benefit from added support structures Adhesives should not be used on the build bed unless further validations with the adhesive have taken place

steps.title: After printing, inspect print for deviations...
steps.instruction: After printing, inspect print for deviations in the print process such as delamination and burrs.

steps.title: Take a quarter-sized amount of rayon fibers
steps.instruction: Take a quarter-sized amount of rayon fibers.

steps.title: Tightly wrap the fibers onto the tip of the swab
steps.instruction: Tightly wrap the fibers onto the tip of the swab. The tip has clear ridges that separate it from the shaft of the swab

steps.title: Extra care should be given to...
steps.instruction: Extra care should be given to ensure the end of the tip is covered. Using a non-white print media can help to see any exposed regions.

steps.title: The swab tip should be 3-4mm...
steps.instruction: The swab tip should be 3-4mm in diameter following the tight wrapping procedure. Excess rayon should be removed or additional rayon should be added to achieve this diameter.

steps.title: Perform non-radial motions to help the...
steps.instruction: Perform non-radial motions to help the fibers intertwine and reduce the chances of the fibers becoming unwound.

steps.title: Pull on the collective rayon fibers...
steps.instruction: Pull on the collective rayon fibers to ensure that the fibers do not become dislodged from the swab. If fibers do become dislodged, ensure wrapping is tight enough

steps.title: Sterilize the swabs utilizing hospital grade...
steps.instruction: Sterilize the swabs utilizing hospital grade high level decontamination at 270 °F for 00:04:00 in individual packages, preferably with a temperature indicator to indicate appropriate temperature has been attained. Prototypes were autoclaved at 121 °C for 00:20:00 without complications.

steps.title: Validation of the effectiveness of printed...
steps.instruction: Validation of the effectiveness of printed nasal swabs can be found in the Guidelines Section.
